source_intake_record.raw_question_text: In the figure to the right, a box moves with speed $5.00 \text{ m/s}$ at the bottom of a rough, fixed inclined plane. The box slides with constant acceleration to the top of the incline as it is being pushed directly to the left with a constant force of $F=240 \text{ N}$. The box, of mass $m=20.0 \text{ kg}$, has a speed of $2.50 \text{ m/s}$ when it reaches the top of the incline. What is the magnitude of the acceleration of the box as it slides up the incline?
extraction_notes: images 字段提供的是 PIL 图像对象字符串表示，不是实际文件路径或 URL

multi_step: the problem is a physics word problem with multiple given quantities (initial speed, final speed, mass, force) that likely must be combined rather than read off directly
the incline diagram likely supplies additional geometric information that must be integrated with the text
the target is a derived numeric quantity (acceleration magnitude), suggesting at least a small chain of reasoning/calculation
verifiability: answer_type is short_text with a single numeric result, which is straightforward to compare against a reference
answer_present=true and the provided answer is a specific magnitude with units
registry_passed=true, so the core assets appear available for downstream checking
